title: Jane Eyre: An Autobiography
author: Brontë, Charlotte
summary: Jane Eyre, an orphaned ten-year-old, narrates childhood scenes at her aunt’s gloomy house where cold weather confines her indoors. Excluded by Mrs. Reed and tormented by cousin John Reed’s bullying, she finds refuge in reading Bewick’s illustrations and imagining distant Arctic coasts. The opening contrasts domestic cruelty and imaginative escape, showing Jane’s humiliation, physical vulnerability, and craving for companionship. Prefatory remarks by the author criticize social hypocrisy and conventional piety, dedicate the work to Thackeray, and argue for distinguishing true religion from outward show. The tone blends moral critique, vivid atmosphere, and early signals of the heroine’s resilient interior life.

characters.name: Jane Eyre
characters.lifespan: Orphaned child living with the Reeds; in the excerpt she endures exclusion and abuse but finds refuge in reading and imagination.

characters.name: John (Jack) Reed
characters.lifespan: Cousin who routinely torments Jane; in the excerpt he strikes and humiliates her and remains at home under his mother's indulgence.

characters.name: Eliza Reed
characters.lifespan: One of Mrs. Reed's daughters; present in the household and participates in excluding Jane.

characters.name: Georgiana Reed
characters.lifespan: One of Mrs. Reed's daughters; present and treated as a favored child.

characters.name: Mrs. Reed
characters.personality: cold, proud, formal, hypocritical toward Jane
characters.lifespan: Aunt and guardian who excludes Jane from family privileges and justifies it; remains the authority figure in the excerpt.

characters.name: Bessie (the nurse)
characters.lifespan: Nursery nurse who tells tales to the children and provides some comfort; present in household life.

characters.name: Mr. Miles
characters.lifespan: Schoolmaster referenced about John Reed's schooling; his opinion is cited about John's health and behaviour.

characters.name: Mr. Reed
characters.lifespan: Deceased father who 'left her none' (no money); referenced as reason given for Jane's dependent status.

characters.name: Bewick
characters.lifespan: Author/illustrator of the volume Jane reads (Bewick's History of British Birds); his engraved vignettes stimulate Jane's imagination.

characters.name: Ahab
characters.personality: referenced as an example in the preface
characters.lifespan: Mentioned in the author's preface as an exemplar in a moral anecdote.

characters.name: Micaiah (son of Imlah)
characters.personality: prophetic figure in the preface's anecdote
characters.lifespan: Referenced in the preface as contrasting with flatterers; used illustratively.

characters.name: Chenaanah
characters.lifespan: Mentioned in the preface as 'son of Chenaanah' (a prophetic contrast); used illustratively.

characters.name: Imlah
characters.lifespan: Referenced in the preface in relation to Micaiah (son of Imlah).

characters.name: W. M. Thackeray
characters.lifespan: Named dedicatee of the second edition in the preface; referenced as a model satirist.

characters.name: Currer Bell
characters.lifespan: Pseudonym under which the book is published and which signs the preface and note to later editions.

characters.name: Pamela
characters.personality: title character from a book Bessie references
characters.lifespan: Mentioned as one of the sources of Bessie's evening tales that entertain the children.

characters.name: Henry, Earl of Moreland
characters.lifespan: Referenced as a source of Bessie's story-telling material (ballad/romance).
